scope.summary: Implement fine-grained vertex and edge-segment translate operations on Panel EdgeShape outlines as CRDT-safe atomic edits (move_vertex, move_edge_segment) beyond the coarse replace-spec-json edit, enabling precise point-level authoring by models and operators
acceptance_criteria: move_vertex(panel_id, edge_index, vertex_role: Start|End, new_pos_cm: [f32;2]) moves the specified vertex; shared vertices between adjacent edges move together
move_edge_segment(panel_id, edge_index, offset_cm: [f32;2]) translates a whole edge segment, dragging both endpoint vertices
Both operations emit TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded with a vertex-level diff (not a full spec_json replace) for efficient CRDT merge
PANEL_CLOSED and SEAM_LENGTH_MATCH re-evaluate after each operation
Model-accessible via edit_panel MCP tool with operation=move_vertex and operation=move_edge
Concurrent model agents moving different vertices on the same panel merge without conflict (different edge indices are disjoint CRDT keys)
scope.risk_if_missed: MT-059 and MT-259 provide CRDT-gated edit_panel that accepts a full panel spec diff, but without sub-edit granularity a model must rewrite the entire panel outline to move one point. This is fragile: a LLM that generates coordinate arrays for 12-vertex panels will introduce subtle errors when told to move vertex 3. Fine-grained vertex operations with validation feedback after each step match how ChatGarment-style self-correction actually works in practice.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
